title: pdfsigner (R package) — guidance for Claude
text: R package digitally signs/verifies PDFs wrapping pure-Rust pdf_signer crate via extendr. Java, OpenSSL, external tools. Submitted CRAN (v0.2.2; see CRAN-SUBMISSION). Renamed signer → pdfsigner avoid case-insensitive CRAN clash Bioconductor’s signeR. repo dir pdfsigner.

title: pdfsigner (R package) — guidance for Claude
text: R/sign.R — public API: sign_pdf() / verify_pdf_signature(). R/extendr-wrappers.R, NAMESPACE — generated; hand-edit. src/rust/ — extendr crate (Cargo.toml lib name pdfsigner, extendr_module! { mod pdfsigner }), src/entrypoint.c (R_init_pdfsigner / ..._extendr), Makevars./ Makevars.win.(libpdfsigner./ -lpdfsigner). src/rust/pdf_signer/ — bundled copy standalone crate (path dep). Keep sync github.com/StrategicProjects/pdf_signer (currently v0.1.7). src/rust/vendor.tar.xz — vendored Rust deps offline CRAN build.

title: pdfsigner (R package) — guidance for Claude
text: pkgdown site auto-built/deployed gh-pages .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml (uses GitHub-installed tidytemplate, pinned SHA) → https://strategicprojects.github.io/pdfsigner/
code: rextendr::document()      # regenerate wrappers + Rd + NAMESPACE (recompiles Rust) devtools::test()          # testthat suite (fixtures in inst/extdata) R CMD build . && R CMD check --as-cran pdfsigner_*.tar.gz

title: pdfsigner (R package) — guidance for Claude
text: extendr module name MUST equal package name (R_init_pdfsigner), src/entrypoint.c must reference R_init_pdfsigner_extendr. regenerate wrappers install time. Makevars must cargo build --lib; running cargo run --bin document R CMD INSTALL breaks Windows/CRAN build (wrappers committed; regen rextendr::document() dev ). Re-vendoring: cd src/rust && cargo vendor vendor && tar -cJf vendor.tar.xz vendor (config vendor-config.toml, identical cargo’s output). macOS local R CMD check shows benign env-messages (linker SDK warning, missing checkbashisms/HTML Tidy) don’t occur CRAN.

what: To sync a new pdf_signer version
title: pdfsigner (R package) — guidance for Claude
text: Copy pdf_signer/src/*.rs → src/rust/pdf_signer/src/ (EXCEPT main.rs — CLI/clap vendored), update bundled Cargo.toml deps, re-vendor.

title: pdfsigner (R package) — guidance for Claude
text: CRAN via devtools::release(); commit CRAN-SUBMISSION record.

title: Authors and Citation
code: Authors and copyright holders of the bundled Rust dependencies =============================================================  The pdfsigner package bundles its compiled Rust backend together with the source code of the Rust crates it depends on (vendored under src/rust/vendor.tar.xz for an offline, network-free build). Each of those crates is the work of its own authors and is distributed under its own permissive open-source licence (MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD, ISC, Zlib, Unicode-3.0, 0BSD, Unlicense or CDLA-Permissive-2.0), all of which permit redistribution within this GPL-3 package.  The full licence text shipped with each crate is preserved inside the vendored sources. what: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: pdfsigner R package digitally sign PDF documents PKCS#12 keystore verify signatures. package powered bundled, pure-Rust backend (pdf_signer crate, wrapped extendr). longer shells Java (BatchPDFSignPortable.jar) Poppler (pdfsig): Java runtime, OpenSSL, external command-line tools required — Rust toolchain install time.

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: Requires Rust toolchain (cargo, rustc) compile bundled backend. Install Rust https://rustup.rs, :

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: PAdES signatures B-LTA via pades_level: "bb" (CAdES signing-certificate-v2), "bt" (+ RFC 3161 signature timestamp), "blt" (+ /DSS certificate chain CRLs), "blta" (+ document timestamp whole file). Levels "bt"+ need tsa_url. Incremental updates: signing appends , earlier signatures stay valid — multi-signature supported. Visible invisible signatures, custom text box + validation link. Cryptographic verification every signature (re-derives signed byte range, checks message digest signer’s RSA signature).

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: visible signature box drawn whenever signtext non-empty; geometry controlled page, x, y, width, height, font_size border. Pass font embed TrueType/OpenType font (WinAnsi/Latin-1 glyphs; default Helvetica) image draw PNG/JPEG logo box. Omit signtext invisible signature.
code: library(pdfsigner)  sign_pdf(   pdf_file          = "input.pdf",   output_file       = "signed.pdf",   keystore_path     = "keystore.p12",      # or env var KEYSTORE_PATH   keystore_password = "password",          # or env var KEY_PASSWORD   signtext          = "Document digitally signed by CastLab",   validate_link     = "https://castlab.org/validate",   reason            = "Approval",   translate         = TRUE,                 # Portuguese date label   font              = "Arial.ttf",          # embed a TrueType/OpenType font   image             = "logo.png",           # draw a PNG/JPEG logo in the box   tsa_url           = "http://timestamp.digicert.com",   pades_level       = "blta"                # bb | bt | blt | blta )

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: entry named list: valid, signer, chain_trusted, covers_whole_document, signed_len, byte_range, detail. Pass roots (PEM file trusted roots, e.g. ICP-Brasil AC Raiz set) validate signer certificate chain; chain_trusted TRUE/FALSE (NA roots given): TSA / CRL endpoints HTTPS supported (bundled Rust backend built https feature, using rustls).
code: result <- verify_pdf_signature("signed.pdf") length(result)                              # number of signatures vapply(result, function(s) s$valid, logical(1)) result[[1]]$signer                          # signer distinguished name verify_pdf_signature("signed.pdf", roots = "icp-brasil-roots.pem")

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
code: openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout key.pem -out cert.pem -days 365 -nodes openssl pkcs12 -export -inkey key.pem -in cert.pem -out keystore.p12

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: R functions call thin extendr bridge links pure-Rust pdf_signer crate. Every Rust dependency vendored src/rust/vendor.tar.xz package builds offline (CRAN policy: SystemRequirements: Cargo, rustc).

title: Digitally Sign and Verify PDF Documents
text: GPL-3. bundled pdf_signer crate vendored Rust dependencies retain licenses.

what: pdfsigner: native PDF signing and verification — pdfsigner-package
title: pdfsigner: native PDF signing and verification — pdfsigner-package
text: Digitally sign PDF documents PKCS#12 keystore verify signatures. heavy lifting done bundled, pure-Rust backend (pdf_signer crate) — Java, OpenSSL, external command-line tools required.

title: pdfsigner: native PDF signing and verification — pdfsigner-package
text: Maintainer: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Authors: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Hugo Vasconcelos hugo.vasconcelos@ufpe.br Diogo Bezerra diogo.bezerra@ufpe.br contributors: Authors vendored Rust crates (see inst/AUTHORS bundled crates licences) [contributor, copyright holder]

what: Digitally sign a PDF document — sign_pdf
title: Digitally sign a PDF document — sign_pdf
text: Signs pdf_file using RSA key + certificate stored PKCS#12 (.p12/.pfx) keystore, writing signed document output_file. signature detached adbe.pkcs7.detached CMS whole document added incremental update, pre-existing signatures stay valid.

title: Digitally sign a PDF document — sign_pdf
code: sign_pdf(   pdf_file,   output_file,   keystore_path = Sys.getenv("KEYSTORE_PATH"),   keystore_password = Sys.getenv("KEY_PASSWORD"),   signtext = NULL,   validate_link = NULL,   reason = NULL,   signer_name = NULL,   page = 1,   x = 36,   y = 36,   width = 320,   height = 64,   font_size = 8,   font = NULL,   image = NULL,   border = TRUE,   translate = FALSE,   tsa_url = NULL,   pades_level = c("bb", "bt", "blt", "blta") )

title: Digitally sign a PDF document — sign_pdf
text: pdf_file Path input PDF. output_file Path signed PDF written. keystore_path Path .p12/.pfx keystore. Defaults KEYSTORE_PATH environment variable. keystore_password Password keystore. Defaults KEY_PASSWORD environment variable. signtext Optional text visible signature box. NULL empty signature invisible. validate_link Optional validation URL appended visible box. reason, signer_name Optional /Reason /Name signature dictionary. page 1-based page number visible box. x, y, width, height Visible box geometry, PDF points (origin page's bottom-left). font_size Font size visible box, points. font Optional path TrueType/OpenType font file (.ttf/.otf) embed visible box. NULL, standard Helvetica used. WinAnsi (Latin-1) glyph range embedded. Ignored invisible signatures. image Optional path PNG JPEG logo drawn visible box. Ignored invisible signatures. border Draw border around visible box. translate TRUE, date label visible box Portuguese; otherwise English. tsa_url Optional RFC 3161 Time-Stamping Authority http:// URL. Required pades_level "bt" . Requires network access. pades_level PAdES conformance level: "bb" (baseline, default), "bt" (+ signature timestamp), "blt" (+ DSS certificates CRLs), "blta" (+ document timestamp whole file). Levels "bt" need tsa_url.

title: Digitally sign a PDF document — sign_pdf
text: Invisibly, path signed PDF. Raises error failure.

title: Digitally sign a PDF document — sign_pdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sign_pdf(   pdf_file = "input.pdf",   output_file = "signed.pdf",   keystore_path = "keystore.p12",   keystore_password = "password",   signtext = "Document digitally signed by CastLab",   validate_link = "https://castlab.org/validate",   translate = TRUE ) } # }

what: Verify the digital signatures of a PDF — verify_pdf_signature
title: Verify the digital signatures of a PDF — verify_pdf_signature
text: Cryptographically verifies every signature pdf_file using bundled Rust backend. signature checked re-deriving signed byte range, confirming messageDigest SHA-256 content validating signer's RSA signature signed attributes.

title: Verify the digital signatures of a PDF — verify_pdf_signature
Verify the digital signatures of a PDF — verify_pdf_signature
text: pdf_file Path PDF verify. roots Optional path PEM file trusted root certificates (e.g. ICP-Brasil AC Raiz set). supplied, signer certificate chain validated roots reported chain_trusted.

title: Verify the digital signatures of a PDF — verify_pdf_signature
text: list one entry per signature. entry named list valid (logical), signer (subject DN), chain_trusted (logical NA roots given), covers_whole_document (logical), signed_len (bytes), byte_range (numeric length-4) detail. length-zero list means signatures found.

title: Verify the digital signatures of a PDF — verify_pdf_signature
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- verify_pdf_signature("signed.pdf", roots = "icp-brasil-roots.pem") vapply(result, function(s) s$valid, logical(1)) } # }
